methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Fervo Energy, a climate and energy company founded by Tim Latimer and Jack Norbeck, is preparing for IPO following $1.5B+ in total fundraising. The valuation exercise examines a fragmented comp universe of nine same-sector peers: one public company (LNZA) and eight private firms including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

The peer-median methodology proved inapplicable due to insufficient clustering. The Climate & Energy sector lacks sufficient companies at Fervo's stage and valuation tier—no three or more comparable companies met narrow filtering criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier). This scarcity prevents defensible median-based calibration; relying on all-sector medians would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneous nature of climate tech subsectors and capital requirements.

Without triangulation anchors from multiples analysis, this valuation cannot be reliably narrowed beyond the known capital raised ($1.5B+). The open questions are substantial: relative revenue or EBITDA multiples versus the single public peer, capital efficiency relative to private comparables, and the degree to which geothermal energy technology commands a risk or growth premium within climate tech. Resolution of these uncertainties requires either expanded peer identification or forward-looking financial modeling tied to production and deployment milestones. Any valuation estimate at this stage would be premature without additional market data or company-specific metrics.
